title: Trust extension in a secure communication framework
abstract: A computer-implemented method, in a communication framework in which each of a plurality of users has one or more devices associated therewith, and in which the users use at least some of their devices to communicate via a backend system. A first user has a first set of first one or more associated trusted devices, and a second user has a second set of second one or more trusted devices associated therewith. The first user forms a first trust relationship between a first device in the first set and a second device in the second set. Based on (i) the first trust relationship, and (ii) the second set associated with the second user, the first user forms a second trust relationship between each device in the first set and each device in the second set. A least one device in the first set communicates with one or more devices in the second set based on the second trust relationship.
claims: Claims 1. A computer-implemented method comprising: (A) forming a first trust relationship between a first device in a first set and a second device in a second set, wherein said first set comprises a first one or more trusted devices associated with a first user of a plurality of users, and wherein said second set comprises a second one or more trusted devices associated with a second user of said plurality of users; (B) based on (i) said first trust relationship, and (ii) said second set associated with said second user, forming a second trust relationship between at least one device in said first set and at least one device in said second set; and (C) at least one device in said first set communicating with one or more devices in said second set, said communicating based on said second trust relationship. 2. The method of claim 1, in which users use at least some of their devices to communicate via a backend system. 3. The method of claims 1 or 2, wherein said first user forms said first trust relationship in (A) and said second trust relationship in (B). 4. The method of claims 1 or 2, wherein, in (B), said second trust relationship is formed between each device in said first set and each device in said second set. 5. The method of claims 1 or 2, further comprising: (D) adding a new device to said first set; and (E) said new device in said first set communicating with one or more devices in said second set, said communicating based on said second trust relationship. 6. The method of claim 5 wherein said adding in (D) comprises said new device forming a trust relationship with at least one device already in said first set. 7. The method of claim 5, further comprising, after adding said new device to said first set in (D): (G) at least one device in said second set communicating with one or more devices in said first set, said communicating based on said second trust relationship. 8. The method of any one of claims 1 or 2, further comprising: (F) removing an existing device from said first set, wherein, in response to said removing, said existing device is removed from said trust relationship with said second set. 9. The method of claim 8, further comprising, after removing said existing device from said first set in (F), (G) at least one device in said second set communicating with one or more devices in said first set, said communicating based on said second trust relationship. 10. The method of claim 2, wherein the first set and the second set are maintained in the backend system. 11. The method of claim 2, wherein at least one set of one or more trusted devices associated with a particular user is maintained as a particular journal in the backend system. 12. The method of claim 11 , wherein each particular set of one or more trusted devices associated with said particular user is maintained as said particular journal in the backend system. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the particular journal associated with a particular user comprises a history of devices associated with the particular user. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein said history of devices associated with the particular user includes a list of (i) at least some devices added to said particular set associated with the particular user; and (ii) at least some devices removed from said particular set associated with the particular user. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein said history of devices associated with the particular user includes a list of (i) all devices added to said particular set associated with the particular user; and (ii) all devices removed from said particular set associated with the particular user. 16. The method of claim 11, wherein the first set of said first one or more trusted devices associated with the first user is maintained as a first journal in the backend system, and wherein the second set of said second one or more trusted devices associated with the second user is maintained as a second journal in the backend system, and wherein, for said communicating in (C), said at least one device in said first set uses the second journal. 17. The method of claim 16, wherein said at least one device in said first set uses information in the second journal to determine which devices of said second user to trust. 18. The method of claim 16, further comprising: (D) at least one device in said second set communicating with one or more devices in said first set, said communicating based on said second trust relationship. 19. The method of claim 18, wherein, for said communicating in (D), said at least one device in said second set uses information in the first journal to determine which devices of said second user to trust. 20. The method of claims 1 or 2, operable in a communication framework in which each of said plurality of users has one or more devices associated therewith. 21. The method of claims 1 or 2, wherein the trusted devices are selected from: a computing device, a computer, a mobile phone, a cellular phone, a tablet computer, a desktop computer, a laptop computer, and a set-top box. 22. An article of manufacture comprising a computer-readable medium having program instructions stored thereon, the program instructions, operable on a device and, when executed by a processor on said device, cause said processor to: perform the method of any one of claims 1-2. 23. The article of manufacture of claim 22, wherein the computer-readable medium is non-transient. 24. A device, including hardware including at least one processor and at least one memory, the device programmed to perform the method of any one of claims 1-2. 25. The device of claim 24, wherein the device is selected from: a computing device, a computer, a mobile phone, a cellular phone, a tablet computer, a desktop computer, a laptop computer, and a set-top box.
